1t home, bulbs, light/temp sensors, etc.

Innovation - Business focus:

[Marr, B. (2021) Business trends in Practice, Wiley (Chapters 12 & 13)](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2705166) – available on VLeBooks

Innovation - NLP focus:

Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 3) – available via Kortext

# Books

# Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision Chapter 4

# Downstream NLP Tasks with Transformers

Transformers show their potential when unleash pretrained models and watch them on downstream Natural Language Understanding(NLU) tasks.

Baseline is how humans do on an NLU task.

### Transduction and the inductive inheritance of transformers

The ability to apply their knowledge to tasks they did not learn. BERT can be trained on sequence to sequence and masked language modelling, and then later fine-tuned to perform downstream tasks that it did not learn from scratch.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### The human intelligence stack

Transduction goes through a trial and error process. It means we take structure we perceive and represent them with patterns/internal state.

# We make representations of the world that we apply to our inductive thinking. Our inductive thinking relies on the quality of our transductions.

A child that normally takes a midday nap, might say I haven't had a nap, so it's not afternoon. The child creates a link between the two events with transduction and makes an inference to generalize and make an induction.

Trained_related events = {sunrise – light,sunset – dark, dark clouds – rain, blue sky – running, food – good, fire – warm, snow – cold}

Transduction

- A child touches a hot stove → burns their hand → creates an internal representation: "Red-hot stove = danger."
- The brain “transduces” the experience into a pattern: avoid red glowing objects.

Induction

- You observe that "Every time it’s cloudy, it rains" (several times).
- You induce a general rule: Cloudy skies usually mean rain.

### The machine intelligence stack

Machines must rely on our random quality language outputs to

perform transductions connecting all the token that occur together in language sequences

Build induction from these transductions

Train those inductions based on tokens to produce patterns of tokens

Transformer model uses self-attention

Attention sub-layers can process millions of examples for their inductive thinking operations

Find patterns through transduction and induction

They memorise these patterns with parameters that are stored with their model

They have acquired language understanding by using their abilities: substantial data volumes, excellent NLP and computer power

### Transformer performance verses Human Baselines

A pretrained model trains on key tasks to enable it to acquire general knowledge of the language. Fine-tuning trains it for downstream tasks.

### Evaluating models with metrics

Accuracy = correct/total

Precision = Tp/ (Tp + Fp)

Recall = Tp/(Tp + Fn)

F1 = 2*(P * R)/(P+R)

# Matthews Correlation Coefficient(MCC)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

# MCC works well even if the sizes of the classes are different.

Tests require

- A model
- A dataset-driven task
- A metric

### GLUE and SuperGlue

General Language Understanding Evaluation(GLUE)

Transformer based models surpassed non-expert humans.

SuperGlue was introduced to set a higher standard for Human Baselines

Site provides software, data set and papers describing the problem.

Choice of Plausible Answers(COPA)  - choose the most plausible answer from a list.

# SuperGlue is to show that a given NLU can perform multiple downstream tasks with fine-tuning

BooIQ - a boolean yes or no answer task. Passage and question

Commitment Bank(CB) is a difficult entailment task. Read a premise then label a hypothesis as an entailment, neutral or contradiction.

Multi-sentence reading comprehension(MultiRC) read text and choose from several possible choices to make(multiple questions).

Reading Comprehension with Commonsense Reading Dataset(ReCoRD) Contains over 120k from 70k news articles. Must use commonsense reasoning to solve this problem.

Recognizing Textual Entailment(RTE)

Read a premise, hypothesis and predict the label of the entailment hypothesis. This requires logical understanding.

Words in Context(WiC)

Determine if the same word in two sentences has the same meaning

Winograd Schema Challenge(WSC)

Each sentence has an occupation, a participant and pronoun. It checks if the pronoun refers to the occupation or the participant

# Running downstream tasks

Corpus of Linguistic Acceptability(CoLA), thousands of sentences annotated for grammatical acceptability.

Label sentences as 0 if not acceptable and 1 if grammatically acceptable.

Standford Sentiment TreeBank(SST-2)

More reviews and sentiment analysis 0 to n

Microsoft Research Paraphrase Corpus(MRPC)

Two sentences that are annotated to whether they are parphrase equivalent or semantic equivalent.

Winograd schemas

Using translation. e.g. English-French, it needs to work out the what the pronoun is referring to since it impacts the gender.

FALSE

True

True

True

True

False

True

True

False

True

Machine intelligence uses the same data as humans to make predictions. (false/)

      SuperGLUE is more difficult than GLUE for NLP models. (True/)

      BoolQ expects a binary answer. (True/)

      WIC stands for Words in Context. (True/)

      Recognizing Textual Entailment (RTE) detects if one sequence entails another sequence. (True/)

      A Winograd schema predicts if a verb is spelled correctly. (/False)

      Transformer models now occupy the top ranks of GLUE and SuperGLUE. (True/)

      Human Baseline standards are not defined once and for all. They were made tougher to attain by SuperGLUE. (True/)

      Transformer models will never beat SuperGLUE human baseline standards. (/False)

      Variants of transformer models have outperformed RNN and CNN models. (True/)

# Main

### Smarter products and services

Smart products transmit key data to react to surroundings or user preferences for meaningful interactions

e.g. home thermostats, smart cars, autonomous ships, intelligent dog doors.

AI can improve the current value proposition for existing services.

AI-as-a-service allows access to AI tools using subscription, such as Oracle's Autonomous Database, which automates security, backups, and other routine tasks.

Or Otter, AI-based business service for generating accurate notes from meetings, interviews, or lectures.

Organisations should consider the benefits of making products and services more intelligent.

### The speed of now

Micro-moments are where a customer wants something now and will turn to their device or app to do so. e.g. directions, shopping, etc.

A business needs to

1. Be there
2. Be useful
3. Be accountable

Can provide personalisation to help customer feel unique. Paying a premium for customisation has been an established trend for some time. e.g. Netflix or google

Need to achieve these outcomes at scale.

Privacy is a risk when it comes to personalisation.

### Pretraining and fine-tuning

We feed in data for natural language understanding(NLU) tasks. We then provide labels and the machine attempts to fit the pattern to a mathematical model. The model created is a best approximation but not perfect.

![Diagram of human and machine learning models as described in the text.](data:image/png;base64,)

Fine-tuning can make a model do something it wasn't initially trained for.

### Transformer performance metrics

### Accuracy - Proportion of correctly predicted instances across all classes.

### F1-score -

Precision(P) = Tp/(Tp + Fp)

Recall(R) = Tp/(Tp + Fn)

F1 = 2*(P*R)/(P +R)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### Matthews Correlation Coefficient(MCC)

![Equation: MCC = TP * TN – FP * FN / Square root of ((TP+FP)(TP+FN)(TN+FP)(TN+FN))](data:image/png;base64,)

A good metric for binary classification models, even if the sizes of the classes are different.

### GLUE and SuperGLUE

General Language Understanding Evaluation

Some tasks to test natural language understanding(NLU)

SuperGlue has more challenging tasks.

SuperGlue has eight tasks that are stricter than in GLUE.

1. CoPA
2. BoolQ
3. Commitment bank
4. Multi-Sentence Reading Comprehension (Multi-RC)
5. Recognising Textual Entailment (RTE)
6. Words in Context (WiC)
7. The Winograd Schema Challenge (WSC)

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/benchmarking-ai/glue-and-superglue/?context_id=15589&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/benchmarking-ai/glue-and-superglue/?context_id=15589&context_resource=lp)>

### CoPA - Choice of Plausible Answer

Choose from the most plausible answer.

Commonsense reasoning about causal relationships.

### BooIQ

Yes or no tasks. Question and passage

### Commitment bank

Examine a hypothesis and premise, then label the hypothesis as neutral, an entailment(logically follows) or contradiction.

### Multi-sentence reading comprehension(MultiRC)

Reads text and then selects from multi choice answer.

Requires understanding across multiple sentences

### Words in Context(WiC)

Process ambiguous words.

Must work out if the same word has the same meaning in different sentences

### The Winograd Schema Challenge(WSC)

Is the pronoun right, in a sentence with a occupation, participant and pronoun.

e.g.

{"text": >"I poured water from the bottle into the cup until it was full.",

Does it refer to the bottle or the cup

## Running downstream tasks

A downstream task is a fine-tuned transformer task that inherits the model and parameters from a pretrained transformer model.

A task is a downstream task if it was not used to fully pretrain the model.

- The human thought process is complex, whilst machines have to learn from our language outputs.
- Important metrics for evaluating NLP models are accuracy, F1-score, and Matthews Corelation Coefficient (MCC).
- To mimic human ability, machines should be able to show language understanding across a variety of tasks.
- GLUE and SuperGLUE are test banks for NLU that contain many tasks.
- Performance in the tasks is measured using accuracy, F1-score, or MCC.
- In this limited sphere of test tasks, transformers can outperform human baselines.
- Pretrained models can be downloaded and fine-tuned for new tasks.
- Fine-tuning is considered to be a downstream task.
- The same tasks can be used for pretraining or fine-tuning.

From <[https://app.qa.com/course/running-downstream-tasks-ai/summary-1738774109119/?context_id=15589&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/course/running-downstream-tasks-ai/summary-1738774109119/?context_id=15589&context_resource=lp)>
